title: Testing a cluster count with matched nulls
text: clustering method applied continuous data report number clusters whether exist. analysis announces dataset contains, say, four types, number may describe population, shape data method. matchednull separates two.

title: Testing a cluster count with matched nulls
text: copula_null() builds synthetic twin data keeps everything innocent removes one thing question: every variable’s distribution preserved exactly (twin reuses real values, reshuffled); correlation matrix preserved within sampling error; remaining dependence Gaussian, twin contains cluster structure construction. pipeline finds many clusters twins real data, clusters data’s shape, people.
code: library(matchednull)  set.seed(1) x <- matrix(rnorm(400 * 3), 400, 3) %*%   chol(matrix(c(1, .5, .3, .5, 1, .4, .3, .4, 1), 3, 3)) twin <- copula_null(x)  all(sort(twin[, 1]) == sort(x[, 1]))   # margins: identical #> [1] TRUE round(cor(x) - cor(twin), 2)           # correlations: close #>      [,1]  [,2]  [,3] #> [1,] 0.00  0.01  0.05 #> [2,] 0.01  0.00 -0.03 #> [3,] 0.05 -0.03  0.00

title: Testing a cluster count with matched nulls
text: matched_null_test() takes data pipeline, wrapped function returns one number (typically selected number clusters). runs identical pipeline real data R twins, asks whether real answer stands . first call returns null-like: whatever clustering pipeline reports reproduced twins types. second returns exceeds null: grouping lives structure twins carry.
code: suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(mclust)) pick_k <- function(d) Mclust(d, G = 1:4, modelNames = "VVV", verbose = FALSE)$G  # 1. Typeless data: the test should stay quiet. set.seed(7) matched_null_test(x, pick_k, R = 30) #> Matched-null test (30 Gaussian null twins) #>   real statistic:      1 #>   null interval:       [1, 1] #>   p (real >= nulls):   1 #>   verdict:             null-like (within the twins' interval) # 2. Two genuine types, hidden in the dependence structure #    (identical margins, opposite correlation orientation). set.seed(42) z <- sample(2, 400, replace = TRUE) X <- matrix(rnorm(400 * 4), 400, 4) L1 <- chol(matrix(c(1, .85, .85, 1), 2, 2)) L2 <- chol(matrix(c(1, -.85, -.85, 1), 2, 2)) X[z == 1, 1:2] <- X[z == 1, 1:2] %*% L1 X[z == 1, 3:4] <- X[z == 1, 3:4] %*% L1 X[z == 2, 1:2] <- X[z == 2, 1:2] %*% L2 X[z == 2, 3:4] <- X[z == 2, 3:4] %*% L2  set.seed(7) matched_null_test(X, pick_k, R = 30) #> Matched-null test (30 Gaussian null twins) #>   real statistic:      2 #>   null interval:       [1, 1] #>   p (real >= nulls):   0.032 #>   verdict:             exceeds the null (beyond margins + covariance)

Check margins first. one regime null flag types separated visible margins covariance (strongly bimodal variables); inspect margins pronounced multimodality relying count test, tie-break granular (Likert-type) scales formal unimodality tests.

title: Testing a cluster count with matched nulls
text: “Exceeds null” Gaussian twins licenses structure beyond margins correlations — types. Heavy-tailed dependence, extreme values across variables arrive together, also exceeds Gaussian null, real questionnaire clinical data heavy-tailed often . separate two readings, rerun test t-copula twins: margins, correlations, tails co-move. ladder reads: result exceeds Gaussian twins t twins hard attribute tails; result t twins reproduce tail dependence, types. Either way verdict sharper single null give.
code: matched_null_test(x, pick_k, R = 200, copula = "t", df = 8)  # moderate tails matched_null_test(x, pick_k, R = 200, copula = "t", df = 3)  # heavy tails

title: Matched-Null Tests for Cluster-Count Claims
text: Documentation: https://haomeng797-ship-.github.io/matchednull/ Clustering methods always give clusters, even data one smooth cloud. makes hard know whether reported clusters reflect real structure simply artifacts method. matchednull helps find . creates null twin dataset exactly marginal distributions approximately correlation structure, cluster structure construction. run clustering pipeline observed data null twins. observed data consistently yield clusters null twins, ’s evidence genuine cluster structure. Otherwise, ’ll know reviewer asks. copula_null() generates null twins, preserving every marginal distribution exactly correlation matrix within sampling error. matched_null_test() applies user-supplied clustering pipeline observed data R null twins, tests whether observed result stands null distribution.

title: Matched-Null Tests for Cluster-Count Claims
code: install.packages("matchednull")  # dev version, has the t-copula stress test remotes::install_github("haomeng797-ship-it/matchednull")

title: Matched-Null Tests for Cluster-Count Claims
text: See vignette("matchednull") full walk-, including case genuine types hide dependence structure test fires. calibration runs skewed clusterless data, standard criteria (BIC, bootstrap LRT) reported structure every dataset. matched-null test flagged none .
code: library(matchednull) library(mclust)  pick_k <- function(d) Mclust(d, G = 1:5, verbose = FALSE)$G  set.seed(1) x <- matrix(rnorm(500 * 4), 500, 4)   # typeless data set.seed(2) matched_null_test(x, pick_k, R = 50) #> Matched-null test (50 Gaussian null twins) #>   real statistic:      1 #>   null interval:       [1, 1] #>   p (real >= nulls):   1 #>   verdict:             null-like (within the twins' interval)

text: Builds synthetic twin x preserves every marginal distribution exactly correlation matrix within sampling error, containing cluster structure construction. copula = "gaussian" (default) dependence twin Gaussian. Clustering found real data twins must come structure beyond margins covariance; clustering found equally never data's shape.

title: Draw one matched null (
text: x numeric matrix data frame (rows = observations, columns = variables), complete cases , least two rows two columns. copula Dependence family twin: "gaussian" (default) "t". df Degrees freedom t copula, single positive number (default 8; used copula = "t"). Smaller heavier-tailed; df = 3 hard stress test, df = 8 moderate one. ridge Small value added diagonal correlation matrix positive definite (default 1e-6).

title: Draw one matched null (
text: numeric matrix dimensions x: one matched-null draw. column contains exactly values corresponding column x, rearranged.

title: Draw one matched null (
text: construction rank reordering tradition Iman Conover (1982): draw Gaussian sample data's correlation matrix, replace column sorted real values laid rank order Gaussian column. Every real value reused exactly per column, margins match exactly; rank correlation matched exactly Pearson correlation within sampling error. copula = "t" construction driven multivariate t sample instead: margins correlations preserved , twin also carries tail dependence, extreme values across variables arrive together, strongly smaller df. t twin still single population clusters. use stress test: verdict "exceeds Gaussian null" t twin reproduces heavy-tailed dependence, types.

title: Draw one matched null (
text: Iman, R. L., & Conover, W. J. (1982). distribution-free approach inducing rank correlation among input variables. Communications Statistics - Simulation Computation, 11(3), 311-334.

title: Draw one matched null (
code: set.seed(1) x <- matrix(rnorm(200 * 3), 200, 3) %*% chol(matrix(c(1, .5, .3,                                                        .5, 1, .4,                                                        .3, .4, 1), 3, 3)) twin <- copula_null(x) # margins identical: all(sort(twin[, 1]) == sort(x[, 1])) #> [1] TRUE # correlations close: round(cor(x) - cor(twin), 2) #>       [,1]  [,2]  [,3] #> [1,]  0.00 -0.02 -0.01 #> [2,] -0.02  0.00  0.03 #> [3,] -0.01  0.03  0.00  # a heavier-tailed twin for stress-testing: stress <- copula_null(x, copula = "t", df = 3) all(sort(stress[, 1]) == sort(x[, 1])) #> [1] TRUE

title: Test a cluster count against matched nulls — matched_null_test
text: Runs user's clustering pipeline real data R matched-null twins (see copula_null()), asks whether real result exceeds twins, share data's margins covariance contain cluster structure, produce. pipeline supplied function, procedure returns number, BIC-selected mixture, k-means heuristic, published typology's workflow, can tested unchanged.

title: Test a cluster count against matched nulls — matched_null_test
code: matched_null_test(   x,   cluster_fn,   R = 200,   probs = c(0.025, 0.975),   copula = c("gaussian", "t"),   df = 8,   ridge = 1e-06 )

title: Test a cluster count against matched nulls — matched_null_test
text: x numeric matrix data frame, complete cases . cluster_fn function taking data matrix returning single number: statistic tested, typically selected number clusters (scalar summary clustering strength works). R Number matched-null twins draw (default 200). probs Lower upper quantiles null distribution used interval verdict (default c(.025, .975)). copula, df Dependence family twins t degrees freedom, passed copula_null(). Defaults "gaussian". ridge Passed copula_null().

title: Test a cluster count against matched nulls — matched_null_test
text: object class "matched_null_test": list real statistic (real), null draws (null), null interval (interval), one-sided Monte Carlo p-value exceedance (p_exceed), interval verdict (within), null family used (copula, df "t"). Reproducibility caller's: set seed calling.

title: Test a cluster count against matched nulls — matched_null_test
text: verdict "exceeds null" default Gaussian twins licenses "structure beyond margins correlations", license types: heavy-tailed dependence also exceeds Gaussian null. separate two, rerun copula = "t" (df 8, 3). result survives t twins well harder attribute tails; result t twins reproduce tail dependence, types.

title: matchednull (development version)
text: copula_null() matched_null_test() gain copula = "t" df: t-copula twins preserve margins correlations add tail dependence. verdict “exceeds Gaussian null” can now stress-tested heavier-tailed dependence (df = 8, 3) read evidence types. matched_null_test() results record null family used ($copula, $df), printed summary names .
